goal: Build a pinned midterm scene every student loads identically — no physics drift, single distributable file.
thoughts: Pedagogy: reproducibility IS the lesson. Past exams broke because default physics timestep can differ by Kit version. Pin dt, substeps, gravity, gpu_pipeline flags in the USD. Export as self-contained package. Ship a sanity-check script that prints a hash of the post-10-step state — grader compares to expected.
code:
# 1. Base scene
load_scene_template(template_name="<SIMPLE_FRANKA_TABLE_STARTER>")

# 2. Add exam cube at EXACT pose
add_reference(prim_path="/World/ExamCube", reference_path="<CUBE_USD>")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/ExamCube", attr_name="xformOp:translate",
              value=[<X>, <Y>, <Z>])

# 3. PIN physics — this is what past exams missed
set_physics_scene_config(
    scene_path="/physicsScene",
    time_steps_per_second=<60>,
    gravity=[0, 0, -9.81],
    gpu_enabled=<True>,
    solver_type="<TGS>",
    substeps=<1>
)

# 4. Self-contained package (no student-specific paths)
export_scene_package(
    stage_path="<EXAM_USD>",
    output_path="<MIDTERM_DELIVERABLE_ZIP>",
    format="zip",
    rewrite_refs_relative=True,
    include_textures=True,
    isaac_sim_version="<5.1.0>"
)

# 5. Sanity check — students paste output, grader compares
run_stage_analysis()
# Expected: N prims, M articulations, physics dt=0.01667
failure_modes: Relying on default physics — drifts across Kit versions
External reference to asset on Jordan's Nucleus — students cannot load
Cube pose via GUI drag — not exactly reproducible
No sanity hash — grader cannot prove student started from identical state
